channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: RFK says 10,000 job cuts necessary at 'sprawling' HHS agency
item.description: Kennedy says HHS has 100 communications departments, 40 procurement departments and “dozens” of IT and human resources units.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Noted economist honored by Trump warns that 25% tariffs risk 'irreparable damage' to US automakers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns in a new analysis that President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on auto imports could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle, adding that the proposed taxes could weaken the ability of U.S. automakers to compete with their foreign counterparts.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Was classified information shared? Senators overseeing military request probe into Signal leak
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The top Republican and Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee requested an investigation Thursday into how Trump national security officials used the Signal app to discuss military strikes and a federal judge said he would order the preservation of the messages, ensuring a
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, AAMER MADHANI and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Saving Billions
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press, banned from White House press pool, renews request to court for reinstatement
item.description: A lawyer for The Associated Press has asked a federal judge once again to reinstate the agency’s access to the White House press pool and some other official events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Walz announces remote work changes for Minnesota state agency employees
item.description: State agency employees will have to work in-person for at least 50 percent of scheduled workdays starting June 1. The policy change provides an exemption for employees who live more than 75 miles away from their primary work location.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: US cities located in states won by Trump would be most hurt by Canadian tariffs, an analysis finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. cities most vulnerable to a trade war with Canada turn out to largely be in the states that helped return Donald Trump to the White House — a sign of the possible political risk he's taking with his tariff plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: Planned trips this week to Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, and a dogsledding race were canceled in favor of a more limited trip to visit American military installations on the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Watch live: Trump speaks at White House event honoring Women's History Month
item.description: "I am especially proud to acknowledge and celebrate the brilliant and talented female trailblazers in my Administration," Trump wrote earlier in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries calls for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries tells NewsNation that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should lose his job.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republicans look to DOGE model for state government efficiency commission
item.description: The commission would consist of a panel of six citizens who would investigate fraud in state agencies and potential conflicts of interest in the Legislature. Three members would be appointed by Republicans and three by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order requiring proof of citizenship in federal elections
item.description: Trump signed an order that directs states to require proof of citizenship when individuals register to vote.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's nominee to run NIH
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed health researcher Jay Bhattacharya as the next leader of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). Bhattacharya was confirmed on a party- line vote, 53 to 47.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Intelligence leaders: We didn’t share classified information in Signal chat group
item.description: Some of the nation’s top intelligence officials will appear before Congress in a pair of hearings this week. Two were participants in a widely criticized war plans group chat on Signal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
